instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
SEO Insertions on What is Loading in Critical Illness Insurance and When is it Applicable? - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: critical illness insurance, critical illness insurance policy
Body Content:
Introduction
Critical illness insurance has become an essential component of every individual’s insurance portfolio since it often enables treatment in case of an individual contracting a critical illness. While health insurance policies are believed to offer relief in terms of meeting medical expenses, the reality is that critical illnesses often require significantly more care than is offered by health insurance policies. Health insurance policies primarily cover hospitalisation expenses but with a critical illness, the patient might require post-operative care, therapy, medicines, etc., all of which might not be covered by a health insurance policy.
However, while opting for a critical illness insurance policy, it is important to understand the different components associated with it. For instance, loading is one of the most critical aspects of having a critical illness insurance plan.
What is Loading in Critical Illness Insurance and When is it Applicable
Loading is an amount levied by insurers most commonly in cases where the policyholder has made any claims in the previous policy tenure. It is charged during the time of policy renewal and the loading amount usually differs from insurer to insurer. There are several different ways in which different insurers compute the loading amount for policyholders’ critical illness insurance policies. Read on below to learn more about them.
Relating to Claims:Loading can sometimes be computed based on the claims you have made in the previous year. Depending on the amount of claims you make, the loading amount will be higher. Alternatively, if you do not raise a claim in the year that loading is applied, the hike in your premium as part of loading could be reduced or even removed.
Relating to Age:Insurance policies of most kinds have a direct relation to the age of the policyholder. This is because if a person is older and closer to retirement age, their ability to pay for their insurance might be hampered. Additionally, for health-related insurance policies, such as with critical illness insurance policies, older people have a higher chance of becoming unwell or contracting a critical illness that may result in the insurer having to pay more for their treatment. To adjust against this risk on the part of insurers, they might charge a loading amount as you age, and your policy comes up for renewal.
Relating to Current Health:The current state of your health has a huge impact on the loading amount you are charged by your insurer while your policy is being renewed. There are certain habits or lifestyles that are risky to your overall health and for which, you might be required to pay higher premiums and be subject to higher loading as well. These could include smoking or constantly participating in adventurous sports that could be dangerous. Such causes could result in a higher loading amount being imposed by the insurer on your critical illness insurance policy because they could lead to a higher chance of you falling critically ill and making claims on the policy. Since the insurer would be required to possibly pay a higher amount for your illness, they improve their chances by increasing the loading amount in your insurance at the time of policy renewal.
Case
Shiv suffered a cardiac arrest at the age of 30. While not too old, doctors suspected his lifestyle had a huge role to play in his suffering a cardiac arrest. He regularly drank as well as smoked while also having a hectic work life and not having the time or inclination to follow a healthy diet.
Thankfully, he had opted for a critical illness insurance policy a few months before. As soon as he was hospitalised due to the cardiac arrest, his family informed the insurer and soon received the lump sum amount required for his treatment, while additionally offering him for monetary compensation for the time that he would be unable to work.
While he was grateful for the financial assistance at the time, nothing prepared him for the shock he received a few months later. When he spoke to the insurer about renewing his policy for the next year, his premium had been hiked by Rs. 10,000. Upon enquiring as to why that had happened, he was informed that it was due to the loading as a result of the claims he had made the previous year.
Conclusion
Loading works as a safety net for the insurer as well as the insured. While it enables the insurer to offset the claims raised by the insured, it also enables high-risk customers to avail critical illness insurance. It also enables an equitable system of premium payments for both high-risk and low-risk customers. If loading was not applied, the same premium would apply to all kinds of customers and that would hardly be fair to the insured persons who are low-risk.
